I shall now expound the possibilities of veins in desert regions.
The water-veins run below the earth's surface in the form of camel's
neck i.e. syphons).
[The camel's neck is crooked, long and thin. The veins of
water in deserts would be very deep and slender,]
gc^ftRT ctm »
If there be an ant-hill to north-east of a Pllu tree, there would
be water at a distance of 4^ cubits to the west of the tree and at a
depth of 25 cubits. The vein of water would be running in a north-
erly direction. The signs would be: in the first Purusa (5 cubits) a
frog, then brown clay, thereafter green clay, then below, a stone
and beneath that water (in plenty).
